errors: [library_incompatible] Gemma4's hybrid attention (different head_dim for sliding_attention=256 vs full_attention=512 with partial_rotary_factor=0.25) is incompatible with auto-round's block-wise quantization calibration approach - RoPE dimensions mismatch at layer 5
RuntimeError: The size of tensor a (512) must match the size of tensor b (256) at non-singleton dimension 3
Error occurs in apply_rotary_pos_emb during block-wise forward pass for layer 5 (first full_attention layer)

Root cause: Gemma4 has hybrid attention with different head_dim for sliding_attention (256) vs full_attention (global_head_dim=512 with partial_rotary_factor=0.25). Auto-round's block-wise quantization does not correctly handle this architectural variation.
solutions: Use a different quantization tool that handles Gemma4's hybrid attention (e.g., directly use GPTQModel, AWQ, or llama.cpp)
Wait for auto-round to add official Gemma4 hybrid attention support
Use a different model variant without hybrid attention
Try quantizing with LLM-Compressor instead of AutoRound
root_cause: Gemma4's hybrid attention (different head_dim for sliding_attention=256 vs full_attention=512 with partial_rotary_factor=0.25) is incompatible with auto-round's block-wise quantization calibration approach - RoPE dimensions mismatch at layer 5
key_error: RuntimeError: The size of tensor a (512) must match the size of tensor b (256) at non-singleton dimension 3 in apply_rotary_pos_emb (transformers/models/gemma4/modeling_gemma4.py L789)
suggested_fix: This is a fundamental architectural incompatibility between Gemma4's hybrid attention and auto-round's calibration method. Use a different quantization tool (GPTQModel, AWQ, or llama.cpp) that handles hybrid attention correctly, or wait for auto-round to add official Gemma4 support.
